description: Add chaos/stress tests to lifecycle, reload, update, and npm-search side panel E2E tests. Covers rapid reload spam, install/uninstall races, and update click deduplication.

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress tests for rapid add/remove config cycling and config write + POST /reload race.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts
Rapid add/remove test writes config adding plugin, then removing, then adding 3x quickly, verifies final state matches
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests config file changes triggering side panel updates via plugins.changed. It uses a local helper `postReload(port, secret)` to send POST /reload to the server. Config changes are done by writeTestConfig + postReload.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with rapid config cycling test. Setup: standard server + extension. Open side panel, verify initial state (E2E Test visible). Then 3x rapidly: remove plugin from config (write config without localPlugins), postReload, 200ms tick, add plugin back (write config with localPlugins), postReload, 200ms tick. After 3 cycles, verify E2E Test plugin is visible in side panel (since last action was add).

Constraints:
- The postReload helper is defined locally in the file — reuse it
- Must wait briefly between config writes for server to process
- The final state should have the plugin visible
- Do NOT modify existing tests

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-reload.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress test for rapid POST /reload spam.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-reload.e2e.ts
Rapid reload test sends 3 POST /reload requests with 200ms between, verifies side panel settles to correct state without double-rendering
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests side panel updates via POST /reload. It has a local helper `postReload(port, secret)` that sends POST to `http://localhost:${port}/reload` with auth header. Tests verify plugin cards appear/disappear after reload.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with rapid reload spam test. Setup: standard server + extension. Open side panel, verify E2E Test visible. Fire 3 postReload calls with 200ms between each (not awaiting in parallel — sequential with short delays). After all 3 complete, wait 1s for side panel to settle, verify E2E Test is still visible and side panel is in clean state (no spinners stuck, no duplicate cards).

Constraints:
- postReload is a local helper — reuse it
- The 200ms delay prevents overwhelming the server but is fast enough to test queueing
- Verify by counting the number of E2E Test plugin cards (should be exactly 1)
- Do NOT modify existing tests

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress test for double-clicking the Update menu item.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts
Double-click test opens the menu, clicks Update, immediately tries to interact with menu again, verifies only one update executes
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests the update indicator dot and Update menu flow. It uses local helpers: `injectOutdated(port, secret, name, latestVersion)` to inject a fake update, and `simulateUpdate(port, secret, name, newVersion)` to simulate a successful update. The update menu item shows a Loader icon while updating.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with double-click test. Setup: standard server + extension. Open side panel, inject outdated state for e2e-test plugin. Open plugin menu, verify Update item visible. Click Update. The menu should close and a loader should appear. Try opening the menu again immediately — verify either the menu doesn't open (button disabled) or the Update item shows loader/disabled state. Wait for update to complete. Verify version text updated.

Constraints:
- The Update menu item is disabled/loading while update is in progress — that's the mechanism preventing double-click
- Use injectOutdated and simulateUpdate helpers from the same file
- Do NOT modify existing tests

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress test for rapid search query spam to verify only the latest query executes.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts
Rapid search spam test types 10 different queries with 50ms between each, verifies search results eventually show results matching only the final query
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests npm search flow — results appear, Install button works. Search has a 400ms debounce. The search input is the main text input at the top. This test file is conditional on npm registry availability (uses npmSlackPluginHasArtifacts helper to skip if npm index not ready).

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with rapid search spam test. Use the same npmSlackPluginHasArtifacts check to skip if npm not available. Setup: standard server + extension. Open side panel. Type 10 queries rapidly: 'a', 'ab', 'abc', 'react', 'slack', 'dis', 'disc', 'disco', 'discord', 'todoist' with 50ms between each. Wait 2s for final debounce to settle. Verify: if AVAILABLE section is visible, the results correspond to the final query (not earlier ones). Clear search and verify clean state.

Constraints:
- Must use test.skip() if npm search is not available (check npmSlackPluginHasArtifacts)
- The 400ms debounce means only the last query (or near-last) should actually trigger a server search
- Don't assert specific npm results — just verify no crash and no zombie results from earlier queries
- Do NOT modify existing tests

userStories.title: Add chaos tests to side-panel-auth-failed.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Add stress test for auth recovery — wrong secret then correct secret.
acceptanceCriteria: New test.describe('stress') block exists in side-panel-auth-failed.e2e.ts
Auth recovery test starts with mismatched secret (shows auth failed), then fixes secret and reloads extension, verifies connected state restores
All stress tests assert zero pageerror events
npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-auth-failed.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-auth-failed.e2e.ts (add stress describe block)

Context:
This file tests the 'Authentication Failed' empty state when extension has wrong secret. It uses createTestConfigDir to create config with mismatched secrets. The auth failed state shows 'Authentication Failed' text.

Changes:
Add test.describe('stress') with auth recovery test. This test is complex — it requires two servers or reconfiguring mid-test. Simpler approach: start with correct server + extension (connected state), verify connected. Then kill server, start new server with DIFFERENT secret on same port. Extension reconnects with old secret → auth failed. Verify 'Authentication Failed' visible. Then kill second server, start third server with CORRECT secret. Extension reconnects → auth succeeds. Verify plugin cards visible again.

Alternative simpler approach: Start with mismatched secret (auth failed shown). Then reconfigure: write correct auth.json, use extension_reload or navigate side panel to trigger reconnect. Verify recovery.

Constraints:
- This is a complex test — the simplest reliable approach is preferred
- The extension auto-reconnects on disconnect with exponential backoff
- Do NOT modify existing tests
- If the test is too complex to implement reliably, a simpler version that just verifies the auth-failed state is stable after rapid page reloads is acceptable
